---
title: "T25 — Volume Operator Extension"
subtitle: "Higher-valence intertwiner spectrum"
---

## Objective

Extend the volume operator Q̂ diagonalization from 4-valent to 5-valent and 6-valent intertwiners. Verify whether the Z₂ sign-flip structure (±q₀ degeneracy) persists.

## Status

🟡 **Ready to implement**

## Theory

The volume operator for an n-valent vertex is:

$$
\hat{Q} = \sum_{i<j<k} \epsilon_{ijk} \, \hat{J}_i \cdot (\hat{J}_j \times \hat{J}_k)
$$

For the 4-valent case with j=1/2, the spectrum shows two-fold degeneracy with ±q₀ pairs.

## Tasks

- [ ] Build intertwiner basis for n=5,6 using ts-quantum
- [ ] Construct Q̂ matrix in singlet subspace
- [ ] Diagonalize and extract spectrum
- [ ] Check Z₂ structure: does ±q₀ persist?
- [ ] Generate figures and Quarto page

## Code

```typescript
import { buildVolumeOperator, diagonalizeSymmetric, checkZ2Structure } from 'timesarrow-numerics';

// Example: 5-valent vertex with j=1/2
const n = 5;
const j = 0.5;
// ... (implementation pending)
```

## References

- Paper: Section 3.2, Eq. (24)
- Supplementary: Corrected basis states |Φ₁⟩, |Φ₂⟩
